name: Pasta W/Szechwan Peanut Dressing
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup peanut butter, smooth or crunchy
1/2 cup hot vegetable stock or water
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to boil and cook the pasta until al dente.
2. While the pasta is cooking, steam the broccoli florets.
3. In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the peanut butter and hot vegetable stock or water until smooth.
4. Stir in the remaining dressing ingredients (soy sauce, rice vinegar, safflower oil, minced garlic, and dry crushed red pepper).
5. When the pasta is done, drain it well.
6. Pour the sauce over the pasta and toss to coat evenly.
7. Add the broccoli and tomatoes; toss again.
8. Garnish with chopped scallions.
Variations: Add more red pepper to taste. Try using Szechwan Peanut Dressing as a warm topping on steamed vegetables, especially green beans, broccoli, or cauliflower. Make extra dressing and refrigerate it for serving later over chilled steamed green beans or broccoli.
